patch.value: validate_history_unknown_author_events: legacy/actionable split for immutable unknown-author history events + first-party automation author coverage

validate/health advisory distinguishes actionable unknown-author events (post-baseline-anchor or recent-window) from the 162 legacy immutable events, which surface as an informational count only; check reports ok when actionable=0; all first-party automation paths (CI gates, Auto Release pipeline, changelog regen, MCP server defaults) are verified to stamp an explicit author; docs state legacy events are permanent by design because history streams are append-only

Why this exists: PR#565 (pm-cpja) shipped scanHistoryAuthorAttribution scanning ALL history with no window (src/sdk/author-attribution.ts). The 162 flagged events predate author discipline and can NEVER be repaired: history streams are append-only immutable proof, so rewriting authors is forbidden by design. Without a legacy/actionable split the warning is permanent noise that violates the every-warning-maps-to-an-actionable-owner invariant. Precedents: pm-2ler (dangling_dependency_references active-only gating with legacy informational count) and pm-e9zh (missing_close_reason legacy set, never hand-backfill). Remediation for NEW events is prevention only: PM_AUTHOR, global --author, or config author_default.
